user_query:
I'm a TV producer prepping next week's expert check‑in for our wilderness survival show. Please build an automated follow-up reminder schedule from the attached inputs, validate it against our production calendar and rules, and generate concise meeting notes with action items.

Inputs (all required):
- input/expert_transcripts.jsonl: expert advice items captured from recent consults.
- input/production_calendar.csv: our phases and blackout weekdays.
- input/contacts.csv: recipient-to-email mapping.
- input/reminder_templates.yaml: subject/body templates keyed by priority.
- input/validation_rules.yaml: business rules for reminder validation.

Deliverables (all required):
1) output/reminders.csv
   - Columns (in this order): id, related_topic, due_date (YYYY-MM-DD or empty), recipient_name, recipient_email, priority, subject, body
   - Build one reminder per transcript line. Use contacts.csv to populate recipient_email; leave recipient_email empty if no match.
   - Use reminder_templates.yaml to generate subject/body. For priorities listed under selection.use_high_priority_for, use the high_priority template; otherwise use the general template. Interpolate tokens {expert}, {recipient}, {topic}, {recommendation}, {due_date}.
   - Do not auto-adjust dates for weekends or phase windows; keep the date from the transcript (or empty if missing). The validator will flag issues.

2) A validator script and report
   - Implement a simple validator at scripts/validate_reminders.py that enforces input/validation_rules.yaml against output/reminders.csv using input/production_calendar.csv and input/contacts.csv.
   - Minimum checks:
     • due_date must be present if rules.require_due_date_present is true.
     • due_date must fall within the phase named by rules.due_date_within_phase (inclusive of start/end) and not on a weekday listed in rules.block_weekdays.
     • recipient must exist in contacts.csv when rules.require_contact_in_contacts is true.
     • subject length must be ≤ rules.subject_max_length.
   - Run this command and capture its stdout to a report: 
     python scripts/validate_reminders.py --reminders output/reminders.csv --calendar input/production_calendar.csv --contacts input/contacts.csv --rules input/validation_rules.yaml > output/validation_report.txt
   - The report must begin with a line in the exact format: "Summary: <n_errors> errors, <n_warnings> warnings" and include a final line with either rules.report.pass_text or rules.report.fail_text. List each error/warning on its own line with the reminder id and a short reason.

3) output/meeting_notes.md
   - Include three sections: "Context", "Expert recommendations and action items", and "Validator findings".
   - In "Expert recommendations and action items", summarize each expert item (id, topic, recommendation, priority) and list an actionable next step with an owner (recipient_name), due date (from due_date or "TBD" if missing), and recipient_email if available.
   - In "Validator findings", quote the first line of output/validation_report.txt (the Summary line) and enumerate each issue from the report as bullets with a proposed follow-up (e.g., "update due date within Pre‑Production", "add missing contact", etc.).

Essential constraints:
- Use only the provided inputs; do not fetch external data.
- The validator must be runnable exactly with the command above and must generate output/validation_report.txt. Analyze the validator’s output: use it to drive the "Validator findings" section and ensure the meeting notes reflect any problems flagged.
- The reminders and notes should be internally consistent with the inputs and templates.

What I expect to receive in the workspace:
- output/reminders.csv populated as specified.
- scripts/validate_reminders.py that runs locally.
- output/validation_report.txt produced by running the validator command.
- output/meeting_notes.md that incorporates the validator’s findings and clear action items. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
